seoTitle: How to View 1099s in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Access and review 1099 forms in QuickBooks Online to prepare for tax season and stay compliant when working with contractors.
intro: Reviewing 1099 forms in QuickBooks Online is an important part of tax season prep for businesses that work with independent contractors or freelancers. This guide walks through locating and reviewing your 1099s so your tax documentation stays accurate.

steps.heading: Navigate to the 1099 section
steps.body: Go to the tax forms area within QuickBooks Online where 1099 information is managed.

steps.heading: Select the tax year to review
steps.body: Choose the relevant fiscal year to see the 1099 data compiled for your contractors.

steps.heading: Review contractor payment totals
steps.body: Check the payment amounts recorded for each contractor to confirm they match your expectations.

steps.heading: Address any discrepancies
steps.body: If a contractor's total looks incorrect, review the underlying transactions and correct any misclassified payments.

steps.heading: Prepare for filing
steps.body: Once totals look accurate, use the reviewed 1099 data to move forward with filing requirements.

faq.question: Who needs to be issued a 1099 form?
faq.answer: Generally, independent contractors and freelancers paid above a certain threshold need a 1099, though specific rules can vary, so it's worth confirming current requirements.

faq.question: Can I fix a payment total before filing 1099s?
faq.answer: Yes, reviewing your 1099 data ahead of filing lets you catch and correct misclassified transactions before submitting anything.

faq.question: Does QuickBooks Online automate 1099 tracking throughout the year?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online tracks contractor payments as they're entered, which is why regularly reviewing this data throughout the year can make tax season much smoother.

faq.question: What happens if a contractor's total looks wrong in my 1099 view?
faq.answer: You'll want to check whether all their payments were correctly categorized as contractor payments rather than miscategorized as another expense type.
